expectations.text: Uses openpyxl to read and modify the existing file
Adds tax column with formula references (=cell*0.10) not hardcoded values
expectations.evidence: Hardcodes: 'ws.cell(r, 2, round(price * 0.10, 2))' -- Python calculation, not Excel formula

expectations.text: Adds a SUM total row at the bottom
expectations.evidence: Hardcodes totals with Python sum(), not =SUM formula
No mention of formula errors or recalc

eval_feedback.overall: Hardcodes values -- the exact anti-pattern the skill warns against
